title: Expressing support for the designation of February 15 through February 22, 2025, as "National FFA Week", recognizing the important role of the National FFA Organization in developing the next generation of leaders who will change the world, and celebrating the 90th anniversary of New Farmers of America and the 75th anniversary of the Federal charter to Future Farmers of America.
plain_summary: This resolution supports making February 15-22, 2025 National FFA Week to honor the Future Farmers of America organization. The FFA helps young people learn about farming and develop leadership skills. The resolution also celebrates two important anniversaries: 90 years since the New Farmers of America was created for Black students, and 75 years since the Future Farmers of America got its official government charter. The New Farmers of America and Future Farmers of America joined together in 1965 to become one organization that serves all students.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.

sections.title: Resolution Supporting National FFA Week
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports making February fifteenth through February twenty-second, two thousand twenty-five, National FFA Week. The House recognizes that the National FFA Organization plays an important role in helping young people become leaders who will change the world. The House celebrates the ninetieth anniversary of New Farmers of America, which was an organization that served Black students in agriculture programs at segregated schools until it joined with FFA in nineteen sixty-five. The House also commemorates the seventy-fifth anniversary of when Congress gave Future Farmers of America a federal charter (official government recognition) in nineteen fifty when President Harry Truman signed the law.

sections.title: Background About FFA
sections.text: The National FFA Organization was started in nineteen twenty-eight. Its mission is to help students reach their potential by teaching them to be leaders, grow as people, and succeed in their careers through agriculture education. Today, FFA has more than one million members in over nine thousand chapters across all fifty states, Puerto Rico, the US Virgin Islands, and Washington DC. FFA welcomes all students. More than fourteen thousand FFA advisors and agriculture teachers provide students with modern and innovative education. FFA gives students experiences that change their lives for the better. FFA members learn skills they need to continue their education in college or start working right after high school. FFA prepares members to be thoughtful citizens of their community, state, country, and the world. FFA provides chances for students to show their reading and writing skills, speak up for important causes, and learn technical skills in farming, food, and natural resources.
